originalBehavior: The published article opens with a scroll-linked introduction where the chart state changes as readers move through staged explanations of lines, residuals, and prediction behavior.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original sticky chart shell, data-index sections, and scroll observer so the compiled scene transitions still drive the visuals in order.
notes: The route still mounts from the original compiled bundle into an otherwise empty document body.
Only metadata and public-shell branding were patched around the app.
evidence: The implementation plan treats scrollytelling step-to-visual transitions as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage scrolls into later steps and waits for the residual overlay state to appear.

originalBehavior: Readers can inspect a fitted line against the scatter plot, see residual markers update, and move into MSE and related loss explanations that recalculate with coefficient changes.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original scatter, residual rectangles and lines, MSE formula updates, and linked coefficient-driven redraws from the compiled app.
notes: The original component IDs and slider wiring were preserved so residual and loss views still recompute from the same stores.
KaTeX rendering continues through the vendored MLU math stack.
evidence: The current plan calls residual or loss visualization parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage changes the bias and weight sliders and verifies the rendered MSE readout changes.

originalBehavior: The article exposes coefficient sliders, tabbed scenes, and prediction or interpretation views that stay synchronized with the currently selected model state.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original manual coefficient controls, tabbed regression scenes, and prediction-linked chart updates without rewriting the compiled logic.
notes: The coefficient sliders still update the same Svelte stores used by the charts and equations.
The bundled resources and references remain where the teaching flow expects them.
evidence: The plan explicitly calls out formula and coefficient synchronization plus pointer-based prediction behavior.
Shared smoke coverage moves at least one coefficient slider and waits for the equation text and chart output to change together.

originalBehavior: The published route included MLU branding, creator bylines, analytics, repo and promo links, and upstream route handoffs inside the public body.
localStatus: The local route removes those public-shell references, localizes the remaining navigation, and uses the shared footer as the single live original-page reference surface.
notes: Docs retain the upstream repo and provenance notes.
The public close keeps educational references but no longer advertises the original project surface.
evidence: The public-surface audit blocks creator names, MLU branding, upstream domains, and promo links in shipped route code.
The route now advertises only the shared footer link back to the live original page.
